def: adj. & s.m. False, &c. = jhūṭ, jhūṭh, q.v.
n. m. 1. Dawn; day break. 2. Dusk; evening twilight.
= H جهوٿا झूटा jhūṭā = jhūṭh, and jhūṭhā, qq.v.
more: adj. False. s. m. Lie, falsehood. جهوٿهـ بولنا jhūṭh bolnā, To lie, to tell a lie, speaking falsehood. جهوٿهـ موٿهـ jhūṭh mūṭh, Lie, lying, falsehood; falsely.
wety: From جُھوٹھ (jhūṭh) – a dated form, inherited from Prakrit 𑀛𑀼𑀝𑁆𑀞 (jhuṭṭha).

def: = H جهوٿ झूट jhūṭ = jhūṭh, and jhūṭhā, qq.v.
more: adj. 1. Liar, false. 2. (among Hindūs) That which has touched food and is thereby defiled. 3. Offal, refuse, left (food); s. m. Leavings of food. جهوٿاجهاٿا jhūṭā jhāṭā, s. m. Offals, left food. جهوٿهاچاٿنا jhūṭhā chāṭnā, To eat leavings, to be very wretched. جهوٿهي نپوچهنا jhūṭhī na pūchhnā, To refuse even common civility.
ex: یہ دھوکے باز نے ہَمیں جُھوٹی مَعلُومات دی۔
ye dhoke bāz ne hamẽ jhūṭī ma'lūmāt dī.
This fraud gave us false information.
تھانے دار نے غَدّار سے جُھوٹے دَسْتاویز پَکَڑ لِئے۔
thāne dār ne ġaddār se jhūṭe dastāvez pakaṛ lie.
The policeman seized counterfeit documents from the traitor.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit जुष्ट (juṣṭa). Compare Punjabi جُھوٹھا /ਝੂਠਾ (jhūṭhā).

def: adj. 1. False; untrue. Mūsā kahat bilār se, sun re jhūṭ jhuṭail, Maiṅ niklā hūṅ sail ko, chhoṛ baiṭh yeh gail . Girdhar Kundalyā. The mouse says to the cat, now hear me, false one, pray, I'm on an outing bent, so get out of my way. 2. Insincere; faithless; inconstant. 3. Unsound; not sound; invalid. 4. Not genuine; counterfeit; imitation. 5. ( jhuṭṭā ) Alloyed; base; debased; not pure; false (lace, etc.). Phr. All is not gold that glitters. 1. Jhūṭā joṛā pahnūṅ maiṅ? annā, yeh jhūṭī bāt hai, Sachche bandoṅ kī pinhā de mujh ko bhārī chūṛyāṅ . Wom. Rangin. What wear false bangles, nurse? all vain. is your essay, The bangles true put on me just, the massive bangles, nay. 2. Dulhan ko sadqe kī guṛyā banāëgā sach much, Yeh jhūṭā goṭā, nigoṛe gaṅvār, kyā hogā? Wom. Rāhat. What dress the bride as though she were a votive doll, This copper lace, you stupid wretch, is of no use at all. 6. Fictitious; invented; forged; artificial. 7. Feigned; hypocritical; pretended; affected; false; sham. 8. Supposititious; imaginary; ideal. 9. Delusive; illusive; illusory. 1. Jhūṭe ghar ko ghar kaheṅ, aur sāṅche ghar ko gor, Ham chale ghar āpne aur log/?/machāveṅ shor . Kabīr. Illusion's home a house is called, one's true home's called a grave, I turned my steps towards my home, with wail the air they clave. 2. Māt, pitā, bhirātā, sukh-dārā, samdhī, sutt, kuṭam parvārā, Rām nām tere saṅg chalegā, aur sab jhūṭā titambā hai . Bhajan. 10. Loose; not tight or fitting; not fixed or firm. 11. Unserviceable ( be-kār ). Hāth jhūṭā ho gayā. jhūṭā baṭ yā paimānā . False weights or measure. jhūṭā banānā yā k. , v. a. To belie; falsify; disprove. jhūṭā paṛnā , v. n. To become loose, weak or unserviceable. Hāth yā kal jhūṭī paṛ gaī . 2. To be proved false; to lose credit with; fall in one's estimation. jhūṭā rupaiyā, yā sikkā , n. m. False or bad coin. jhūṭā kāgaz, yā dastāvez , n. A forged or false document. jhūṭā kāgaz banānā , v. a. To fabricate; forge. jhūṭā lapāṭī , n. m. A great liar. Kiskā hai koī āshia? jhūṭe haiṅ, lapāṭī haiṅ; Kahte ho yeh tum, sāhib, bande ke sunāne ko . Where lives a lover true? Your words are false I fear, Vain trifles, sir, meant just to please your servant's ear. jhūṭī khabar denā , v. a. To misinform; give false intelligence. jhūṭī saugand, yā qasam , n. f. A false oath; perjury. jhūṭī gavāhī, yā sākshī , n. f. False testimony or evidence; false deposition. jhūṭī nālish , n. f. jhūṭā muqadmā , n. m. Groundless or idle suit; false charge or complaint. jhūṭā , n. m. A liar; story-teller. 1. Pahle jhūṭoṅ ko maut ātī thī, ab bukhār bhī nahīṅ ātā . Prov. Time was when the liar was struck dead, now he does not even catch a fever. 2. Jhūṭe ke āge sachchā ro mare . Prov. Truth weeps and dies before the false man's lies. 3. Jhūṭhe ke mūṅh meṅ bū ātī hai . Prov. The liar's mouth stinks. jhūṭoṅ kā bādshāh, sau jhūṭoṅ kā ek jhūṭā , A great liar; a king of liars.
more: adj. Touched; defiled. Jhūṭā khāīye mīṭhe ke lālach . Prov. He eats defiled meats because they are sweet.

n. m. 1. A swing; oscillation; nodding. 2. (S. जटा ) The long hair of a woman's head. Jhoṭe upār lūṅgī . Wom. 3. ( jhoṭī , n. f.) A young buffalo. Jhoṭe jhoṭe ṭakkareṅ laṛeṅ, jhuḍiyoṅ kā nuqsān . Prov. When buffalos fight the plantation is ruined. jhoṭam jhāṭā ; Rus. jhūṭ-pāṛ , n. m. Wom. Pulling one another by the hair of the head.

def: s.m.f. Left, rejected, stale; — a stale thing; a cast-off woman; leavings, &c. ( = jhūṭā).
n. f. H. jhūṭā leavings. A stale (woman).

v.t. = jhuṭālnā, q.v. v.t. To pollute victuals by touching or tasting; to touch or taste (food); to leave (remains of food); — jhuṭālnā, v.t. def: v. a. H. jhūṭ pollution. 1. To pollute victuals by touching or tasting. 2. To taste or touch (as food) ( biṭālnā ).

v. a. To disprove, to falsify.

s.m. Life, soul, self, spirit, mind; heart; courage; disposition; affection, regard; strength, health; — any living thing; — a form of address, a term of endearment or respect, Sir, Master, Madam; — adv. Yes Sir, yes; true; very good ( = jī hāṅ)
more: intj. 1. Sir; master; madam. 2. An honorific form of address, as Esquire. Jī kaho, jī kahlāo . Prov. Respectful your address, from others you will get no less. jī hāṅ, jī , intj. Yes Sir; true; quite so; very good.

n. m. S. जीव, Pr. jīviam; jiam . 1. ( jīvṛā ) Life ( jān ); soul; self; spirit; mind; conscience. 1. Jī ke badle jī . Prov. A life for a life. 2. Hamārā jī gavāhī detā hai . 2. Any living thing; insect; worm; animal. Jī kā bairī jī . Prov. Life preys on life. 3. ( jivṛā ) Disposition; temperament; inclination. Āp kā jī to achchhā hai? I hope you are well. 4. Health; good health Jī hai to jahān hai . Prov. While health is yours the world is yours. 5. Courage; energy; strength. 6. A form of address to oneself. Chal jī, ise bhī kar le . jī uṭhānā , v. a. To turn away one's affections. jī ā jānā, dil ā jānā , v. n. To fall in love. 1. Gadhī par jī ā gayā to parī kyā hai? What's a fairy to him who is in love with a donkey? 2. A gayā jī kisī pe, jī hī to hai; Lag gaī āṅkh, ādmī hī to hai . Mīr. jī uṛā jānā , v. n. To be confounded, dazed, bewildered. jī uktānā , v. n. To be tired, disgusted, c…

def: n. f. S. जिव्हा Pr. jīhā , Pālī, jivhā . The tongue ( zabān ); the organ of speech. 1. Ahmad! jab lag kar chale, tab lag jībh chalāë, Jībh chale, kar nā chale, wohī jībh jal jāë . Ahmad. Ahmad! while you have power to do, your tongue may go, Fire burn the tongue which brags but nothing e'er can do! 2. Jab bolo jab 'Rām hī Rām,' Khālī jibhiyā kaunhū kām? Bhajan. Hymn. Whene'er you speak 'Rām! Rām!' but say, What boots a silent tongue I pray? jībh karnā , v. a. 1 To speak; answer; open one's mouth. 2. To be saucy or insolent; to abuse. Chūṅ jībh kare saë? Rus. jībh ke tale jībh h. , v. n. To be double-tongued. jībh nikālnā , v. a. 1. To put out one's tongue. 2. To feel the heat; to pant. 3. To pull out the tongue (a punishment for misusing it).

To silence, to interrupt oneʼs discourse. 2. To forbid by signs. 2. (from the Persian) To grant the request of a petitioner. 3. To be struck with terrour or astonishment. جيبهـ نکالنا jībh nikālnā, To be extremely fatigued or thirsty.

r: jīm, Urdū, Hindūstānī, Nāgarī, j, judge, abjad, jamādaʼl-āḵẖir
def: The seventh letter of the Urdū or Hindūstānī, and the eighth consonant of the Nāgarī alphabet. It has the sound of the English j as in judge. In the abjad, q.v., it has the numerical value of 3. In almanacs it stands for Tuesday; in astronomy for the sign Cancer. It may also stand as an abbreviation for the Arabic month jamādaʼl-āḵẖir.
more: the fifth letter of the Arabic, the sixth of the Persian, the seventh of the Hindustānī, and the eighth of the Hindī and Sanskrit alphabets. In A. abjad , q. v. it stands for the number (3); for Tuesday in almanacs, and for the sign Cancer in Astronomy. It is interchangeable as initial, medial, and final with g , as jānā = S. gam to go; jān = gyān knowledge; bhījnā = bhīgnā to be wet; bhājnā = bhāgnā to run; bhanj = bhaṅg to break: with d , as jahez = dahez dowry; jhāṅsā = dhāṅsā hoax; jījī = dīdi sister; Jasrat = Dasrat; Jasodhā = Dasodhā; aj = S. adya to-day; jujh = judh fight; kāgaj = kāgad paper: with y , as jag = yag feast; jug = yug period; jatan = yatan scheme; jantr = yantr a machine; rājā = rāyā a king; āj = Tir. āī today: with ch , as jhaṭpaṭi = chhaṭpaṭi haste; jhalnī = chhalnī a sieve; jhijaknā = jhichaknā to be startled; ganjfā = ganchhuā card; būjhnā = pūchhnā to ask; kunjī…

the fifth letter of the Arabic and the sixth of the Persian alphabet, has the sound of j in the English word judge. In Sanskrit, its correspondent and equivalent letter is ज : and, to represent the aspirated letter झ jh, it is combined with ه thus جهـ. In Arabic, it stands for 3 in the abjad numeration, for Tuesday in almanacks, and for the sign Cancer in astronomy: it may, also, stand as an abbreviation for the month, جمادي الاخر It is sometimes used for ش as کاج for کاش Would! for ز as چوجه for چوزه A chicken; for گ (particularly in conformity to Arabick pronunciation), as فرنج for فرنگ Europe; for ه as فيروزج for فيروزه A Turquoise. Again, for ج are used, ت as تارات for تاراج Plunder; ژ as کژدم for کجدم A scorpion; گ as اخشيگ for اخشيج Contrary. Rarely it is redundant, as ديباج for ديبا Brocade. In words borrowed from the Sanskrit, ज is generally substituted for य which is pronounced …

ety: S. यस्य, gen. of यः, base यद्; Prk. जस्स, with the fem. form ji instead of ja, for the base
def: adv. Upon which, whereupon
more: rel. pron. S. यत् Oblique case of jo . Whom; which; that; what. jis par . On which; whereupon. jis par bhī . Notwithstanding; withal. jis tis . Whoever; whatever; some one or other. Jis tis ko lūṭ-kar apnā pot pūrā kar hī letā hai. jis jis . Whichever; each of which; whoever. jis jagah, jis jis jagah, jis muqām par, jis mauqe par, jīs jānib . Wherever; where. jis dam, jis vaqt . When; while; whilst ( /?/abki ). jis tarah , adv. According to; as. jis tarah ho yā ho sake, jis tarah mumkin ho , adv. At any rate; any how. jis taraf , Wherever; whithersoever; in whichever direction. jis qadar , adj. As much as; to what degree. jiskā, jiskī, jiske ; Panj. jedā, jisdā; Bhoj. jekr ; Tir. jakar ; S. यस्प Pr. jissā, jīse, jīā, jīë, jū , Whose; of whom. Ekar bāp jakkar sasur, takkar bāp hamrā sasur . Tir. Riddle. Ans. Sister and brother. This one's father is that one's (my husband's) father-in-law,…

Where, wherever. جس دم jis dam, adv. When, while, whilst. جس طرح jis t̤araḥ, adv. According to, as. جس طرف jis t̤araf, adv. Wherever. جسقدر jis ḳadr, adv. n. f. S. जि to conquer. 1. See jaë . 2. Gain; profit. 1. Do hī chīz haiṅ, hār yā jit . Prov. It's one of two, lose or win. 2. Jīt kī havā bhī achchhī . Prov. Even the name of success is worth having. 3. Divālī jīt, sāl bhar jīt . Prov. Win at Divālī, win all the year round. 3. Advantage; success; superiority; the upper hand. jīt-patr , n. m. s. m. 1. Name of a plant. (Ӕschynomene sesban). 2. Name of a musical mode.

ety: Prk. जीवंतओ; S. जीवन्त+कः; or imperf. part. of jīnā
def: adj. (f. -ī), Living, alive
more: adj. H. jīnā to live. 1. Alive; living ( zindah ). 1. Mujhe mile to jīte ko khā jāūṅ . Mah. Wom. If I only get at her I'll eat her alive. 2. Jītā nākhūn mat lenā . Dont cut the quick of my nails. 2. Something over and above; more. Zarā jītā tolo. jītā jāgtā, jīnā jāgnā , adj. Alive and well; living; healthy; prosperous. Jītā jāgtā beṭā ho! Old women's blessing. jītā jhūṭ , n. m. A gross or notorious falsehood. jītā chamṛā, yā mās , n. m. Living flesh. jītā chamṛā chīrnā , ( Slang .) v. n. See bikr toṛnā. jītā rahnā , v. n. To live; live long; outlive. jītā gāṛnā , v. a. To bury alive. Gālī degā to jītā gāṛ dūṅgā. jīte jī ; A. hīn hayāt ; P. tā ba-zindgī , adv. For life; during one's life-time. 1. Sab jīte jī kā jhagṛā hai, yeh terā hai, yeh merā hai; Yeh dam jis dam nikaltā hai, na merā hai, na terā hai . Faqir Chand. All struggle and strife belong to life; this one is yours, that one is…
adj. 1. (for जीवित ) Alive, living. جيتي جي jīte jī, adj. Alive, living. 2. (for जेता ) Over, more, above. جيتاهونا jītā honā, To be over, to exceed.
subs: adj. Living and healthy; alive and well, alive and flourishing
v.n. To remain or continue alive; to enjoy a long life

def: n. f. S. ज्येष्ठपत्नी The husband's elder brother's wife. 1. Maiṅ soī thī sukh nīṅd, pīyā ko koko legaī rī, Jiṭhānī tū bhī āīyo rī, daurānī tū bhī āīyo rī . Wom. song. (Spend much, enjoy much). 2. Jitnā chhāno utnā hī kirkirā . Prov. The more you strain the more grit you get. (The closer you examine the more faults you find). jitne meṅ , adv. Inasmuch as; on which.

def: v.t. To win; to conquer, vanquish, overpower, overcome, defeat, beat; to subdue; to suppress; — v.n. To be victorious (over, -se); to succeed
more: v. a. S. जि to conquer, Pr. jinai . 1. To win; conquer; succeed. 1. Bāzī jīt lī . 2. Tum se kaun jītegā? No one can get the better of you. 3. Jag meṅ Rām bhaje so hī jītā . Bhajan. Who worship Rām shall conquer. (Compare—Who in the strength of Jesus trusts Is more than conqueror. Hymn.) 4. Jītā so hārā, aur hārā so muā . Prov. Who wins loses, who loses dies. 2. To beat; defeat; have the advantage; prevail; overcome; carry a point; triumph. Muqadmā jīt gayā .
v. a. To win, to conquer, to overcome, beat.

def: s.f. = jihvā, q.v. s.f. The tongue ( = jībh)
subs: s.m. 'Tasting with the tongue,' licking, lapping
s.m. The fur of the tongue.

def: adj. & s.m. Living, alive, animate; living (upon or by); — a living being.
s.f. Means of subsistence, livelihood, maintenance, allowance, stipend, pension; occupation or profession (by which a subsistence is obtained).
adj. & s.m. Living, alive, existent — living, life, existence; duration of life; livelihood, &c. ( = jīvan)
subs: s.m. Life-time, period or duration of life, a life
(jīvita+īśā), s.m. 'The lord or master of existence'; a lover; a husband; an epithet of Yama the regent of death; a drug to revive the dead.

def: adj. Living, alive.
v.n. To live ( = jīnā, q.v.). v.n. To live, be alive, to exist, to subsist; to enjoy life; to be revived, be restored (to health, &c.); — s.m. Living; life
subs: v.n. To rise up alive, to be resuscitated, to come back to life again, to be revived, to get new life.

def: s.f. A feast, entertainment, &c. = jeʼonār, q.v.

n. m. 1. Life; soul. Gayā ho jab apnā hī jivṛā nikal; Kahāṅ kī rubāī, kahāṅ kī gazal? Mīr Hasan. The breath of life once ebbed away, What boots the sonnet or the lay? 2. Soul; sweetheart; beloved. 3. Will; desire; inclination. 4. Half-yearly payment of grain for wages to the washerman. sailānī jīvṛā , n. m. A roving character. nir-jīū , adj. Lifeless; inanimate; defunct.
more: n. m. A rope ( rassā ). Jevṛe se nāṛā ( nāṛ ) ghisnā hai . Prov. Cattle and wives must rub along in their tethers.

def: s.m. = jīvṛā, or jiyūṛā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀚𑁄𑀟 (joḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-); see there for more.

def: s.m. Life, soul; beloved object, sweetheart; will, pleasure, wish; — subsistence allowance, or annual payment (generally in grain) to village washermen and others.
more: s. m. 1. ( जीव ) Life, soul, sweetheart, beloved. 2. ( जीविका ) Annual wages to washermen and others.

def: s.f. = jītiyā, q.v. s.f. Name of a fast observed by Hindū women who have lost children, with the view of preserving the remainder. (In this fast the goddess Devī is sacrificed to and worshipped)
s.m. Life, soul ( = jī); beloved, dear, darling.
more: n. m. 1. Breath; life ( jī ). Āj jīyā meṅ more bas gaī re Sāṅvaryā kī lachak chāl . Wom. song. Ṭhumrī . 2. Dear ( piyārā ); mother; sister.

def: s.m. pl. of next, q.v.
v.t. = jilānā, q.v. v.t. To give life to, cause to live, to vivify, animate; to restore to life; to allow to live, to preserve alive; to foster, to patronize
more: v. a. To cause to live.
subs: v.t. To restore to life; to revive.

def: v.t. (Braj) = jiyānā; jilānā; q.v. v.t. = jilānā, q.v. v.t. To give life to, cause to live, to vivify, animate; to restore to life; to allow to live, to preserve alive; to foster, to patronize
more: v. a. (in Braj) 1. To cause to live, to recover any one almost dead. 2. To foster, to patronise.
subs: v.t. To restore to life; to revive.

def: s.m. (poet.) = jiyā or jī, and jiyarā, qq.v. s.m. Life, soul ( = jī); beloved, dear, darling.

ety: S. जीव+rā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. Life, soul; beloved, dear, darling ( = jīvṛā).
more: s. m. Life, soul; ( met. ) beloved.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀻𑀭𑀕 (jīraga), from Sanskrit जीरक (jī́raka). Doublet of زِیرَہ (zīra). Cognates include Assamese জীৰা (zira), Bengali জিরা (jira), Dhivehi ދިރި (diri), Gujarati જીરું (jīrũ), Marathi जिरीं (jirī̃), Nepali जिरो (jiro), Odia ଜୀର (jira), Punjabi جيرا (jīrā), Sindhi جيِرو (jīro), and Sinhalese දුරු (duru)

def: s.m. = jītab or jītav, q.v. s.m. Life, existence ( = jīvan).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit जित (jita). Compare Punjabi جِتّ (jitt).

def: adj. Offended, displeased, put out
more: adj. Offended; displeased. jizbiz h. , v. n. To be unable to retaliate or avenge oneself; not to know what to do.
subs: v.n. To fume, fret, rave helplessly.

ety: for A. جزية jizyat, fem.; rt. جزي 'to give as a satisfaction,' &c.
def: s.m. A capitation-tax levied on the non-Muslim subjects of a Mohammadan government; (local) a house-tax on the inhabitants of towns not engaged in tillage.
more: n. m. جزي made satisfaction. A capitation tax levied by Mahommedans on infidels. In Sāgar a house-tax on the inhabitants of towns not engaged in tillage.
s. m. A tribute, capitation-tax levied on individuals, poll-tax.

def: s.m. Knowledge; understanding, intellect, intelligence; sacred or religious knowledge (such as is derived from meditation on the higher truths of religion and philosophy, and which teaches man his own nature, and how he may be reunited to the supreme spirit); cognizance; consciousness
subs: (jnāna+adh˚), s.m. Superiority or pre-eminence in intelligence
s.f. The pursuit or practice of wisdom
s.m. The eye of intelligence, mind's eye; intellectual vision
s.m. 'Mirror of true knowledge'; a man of Manjuśrī, one of the Jain saints
s.f. Clear vision or perception (untainted either by ignorance or by sin)
s.f. A state of intelligence or knowledge
s.m. That inner or esoteric portion of the Veda which relates to true spiritual knowledge or the knowledge of supreme spirit as distinguished from the knowledge of ceremonies
jnān-vān, adj. & s.m. Endowed with knowledge or science; knowing, familiar with, intelligent, wise, learned; having spiritual knowledge; — one who understands fully, &c. ( = jnānī, q.v.)
s.m. The thing called jnān, q.v.
s.m. Sacred and miscellaneous knowledge; the Vedas with the supplementary branches of knowledge, medicine, arms, &c.
(jnāna+ić˚), adj. Desirous of knowledge or wisdom
(jnāna+in˚), s.m. An organ of perception or sensation (of which there are five, viz., the skin, tongue, eye, ear, and nose); mental power, intellect.

def: adj. & s.m. Endowed with knowledge or intelligence, intelligent, wise, knowing, learned, sage, judicious; — one who understands fully; one endowed with religious knowledge; one who knows future events; a sage; a prognosticator, astrologer, fortune-teller (syn. jnān-wān).

adj. & s.m. Endowed with knowledge or intelligence, intelligent, wise, knowing, learned, sage, judicious; — one who understands fully; one endowed with religious knowledge; one who knows future events; a sage; a prognosticator, astrologer, fortune-teller (syn. jnān-wān).

s.m. Making known, announcing, notifying, apprizing, informing; teaching; announcement, notification, information
subs: adv. For the purpose of apprizing, &c.

def: part. Made known, informed, notified; revealed; discovered.
part. Made known, informed, taught, expounded.
s.f. Knowing; understanding, apprehension, comprehension; the exercise of the intellectual faculty.
adj. & s.m. Knowing, wise, intelligent; acquainted (with); — one who knows, a knower, a wise or intelligent person; an acquaintance.
part. Known, ascertained, comprehended, understood
subs: (jnāta+an˚), s.m. 'Of known lineage'; a name of Vardhamān, the last Jin or Jain pontiff
s.m. A man completely versed in any science or śāstra.

def: adj. To be known or understood; to be investigated or inquired into; perceptible; conceivable; to be considered as.
s.m. A father; a paternal relation; a kinsman; a distant kinsman (one who does not participate in the oblations offered to deceased ancestors).
adj. & s.m. Knowing, wise, intelligent; acquainted (with); — one who knows, a knower, a wise or intelligent person; an acquaintance.

ety: Prk. जेम्व or जेंव, fr. S. यावत्
def: adv. When ( = jab, q.v.)
subs: adv. (correl. wǒhīṅ), The very moment that, as soon as, no sooner.

def: conj. If, if that, that; in that, inasmuch, since
subs: Though, although ( = agarći); inasmuch as, whereas ( = ćūṅki).

ety: Ap. Prk. जेवडु (by elision of ड or change of ड to ण); Prk.जेद्दहो; S. याट्टश+कः
def: pron. Who, which, that, what
more: pron. rel. S. यः Pr. je . 1. Who; what; which; that. 2. (S. यदि ) conj. If. jo jo , adv. Whoever; whatever; whichever. jo chīz , What thing; whatever; whatsoever. jo kabhī , adv. If ever. jo kuchh ; S. यन्किञ्चित् Whatever; whatsoever; any. jo koī ; Bhoj. je keh ; S. यः कः Whoever; any. jo ki . Though; although ( agarche ); in asmuch as; whereas ( chūṅke ); seeing that. jo na lagtā ho, jo na miltā ho , What is inapplicable, or inappropriate. jo ho , Whatever. Jo ho so le āo. jo ho saktā hai . What is practicable or possible; what can be done. jo ho so ho . Come or happen what may.
(or jū ) 1. s. f. A river, a brook: see جوي joʼe. 2. part. act. (from جستن) used in compos. Seeking, seeker.

1. ( यःयद् &c.) pron. rel. Who, what, which, that. 2. or jau (for यदि ) conj. If. 3. jo or jau (for यदा ) adv. When: see جب jab. جوجو jo jo ( योयः ) Whoever, whatever. جوچيز jo chīz, What thing, whatever. جوکچهـ jo kuchh ( यत्किंचित् ) Whatever. جوکوٴي jo koʼī ( यःकोपि ) Whoever. جوکه jo-ki, Though, although, whereas, whichever. جولون jau-lauṅ, As long as: see جب jab.
subs: The thing which; whatever thing, whatever
Whatever happens; at all events, at any rate
Whatever can be done, what is practicable or possible

def: s.m. Youth, youthfulness, the prime of life, bloom of youth; puberty, manhood, adolescence; vigour, bloom, flourishing state; the pleasures of youth; exquisite enjoyment; the pleasures of sense, taste, or culture; (met.) the breast, well-developed breasts; a fine figure; beauty
more: n. m. S. यौवनम् Pr. jovvanam youth. See بـﮩـار 3, 4, 5. Baṛā joban ā rahā hai . 1. Puberty; adolescence; prime; youth; bloom. 1. Javānī meṅ gadhī ke bachche par bhī joban hotā hai . Prov. Even a donkey young is fair to see. 2. Kyā karūṅ morī māṅ? Morā naval jobanā yoṅ hī jāë; Lākh kahūṅ, morī mānat nāhīṅ, Jūṅ jūṅ manāūṅ voh to rūṅso hī jāë . Wom. Rus. song. O mother dear, what shall I do? My blooming youth is wasting so, Whate'er I say he 'll nothing heed, But angry gets the more I plead. 2. Well-developed breasts; a fine bust. Maiṅ kaise ke niksūṅ aṅ ganvā, Morā deorā nihāre jubanvā? Wom. Song. Thumrī. How can I in the area go! My brother-in-law stares at my bust so! 3. A fine figure; rounded charms; beauty. 1. Chhab gathṛī meṅ, joban rakābī meṅ . Prov. Fine show in dress, plumpness in the mess. 2. Nīm kā joban nīm nibaulī, ām kā joban aṅbyāṅ, Gorī kā joban pān phūl, panihāri kā joban k…
s. m. 1. Puberty, adolescence, prime, youth. 2. ( met. ) Breast.
subs: joban umaknā, joban umaṅdnā, v.n. Puberty to show itself; the breasts (of a woman) to be developed
To attain to puberty; to be in full vigour; to bloom
s.m. Youthful strength or vigour; — adj. Strong with the strength of youth
m. (joban-kī mātī, f.) adj. Intoxicated by (the pride of) youth
v.n. To taste or enjoy the pleasures of youth to the full, to revel in the pleasures of youth
m., joban-vatī, f. adj. Young, youthful, juvenile; attained to or arrived at the age of puberty, fit to be married, marriageable; beautiful, handsome.

def: s.m. = joban.
adj. S. योवनवत् Pr. jovanavanta youthful. 1. Young; youthful; juvenile. 2. Beautiful; handsome.
adj. f. At the age of puberty, fit to be married, youthful.
v.t. (prov.) = jovnā; johnā, qq.v. v.t. To see, look at, behold, regard.
s.m. A fighter, soldier, warrior, combatant; a brave fellow, a hero.
more: n. m. S. योध्दा A warrior; combatant; hero; champion; knight. 1. Sukh ke baṛe jodhā-rakhvālī haiṅ . Wom. Prov. The jewel happiness is guarded by great warriors bold. 2. Larat akāsh yugal sam jodhā, Ekāhīṅ ek hanat kari krodhā . Rāmāyan.

def: s.m. Fignting; battle.

ety: jodhā+Prk. प्पणं = त्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Soldiering; bravery, prowess, heroism.
s.m. A warrior &c. = jodhā.
s. f. A wife.
more: s. f. A rivulet, a brook. جويبار joʼebār, A large river formed of many smaller streams. ¶ jo-ī (from جستن) in compos. s. f. Seeking, searching.

def: n. f. S. जाया A wife. 1. Ghar meṅ āī joe, ṭeṛhī pagṛī sīdhī hoë . Prov. When the wife's brought home you then shall see The turban awry soon straightened will be. 2. Āsan driḍh, āhār driḍh, sumati gyān driḍh hoë; Tulsī! binā upāsnā bin dūlhe kī joë . Through meditation close and self command right mind will come, Tulsī! who worships not is as a bride without bridegroom. 3. Din kī bahan, rāt kī joë, Waqt paṛe mahtārī hoë . Riddle. Ans. A sword. A sister by your side, a wife by night, Provoke her and she is a mother fierce in fight.
s.f. A wife ( = jorū).

s.m. Joining, uniting, fitting; connection, union; yoking; putting to (horses); meeting, contact; mixture, combination, association; method, means, mode, manner; propriety, fitness, suitability; fixing (an arrow or other weapon); application of a remedy, remedy, cure; consequence, result; occasion, opportunity; (in Astron.) conjunction, a lucky conjuncture, auspicious or fortunate moment; (in Arith.) addition, sum, total; — application or concentration of the thoughts (keeping the body in a fixed posture); abstract contemplation, meditation, devotion, penance; union with the Supreme Being by means of abstract contemplation; the Yoga system of philosophy (as established by Patanjali, to teach the means by which the human soul may attain complete union with the Supreme Being); — the twenty-seventh part of a circle measured on the plane of the Ecliptic (and used in calculating the longitudes of the sun and moon); a division of the moon's path corresponding to the twenty-eight Nakshatras; (in banking) the person on whom a draft or bill of exchange is drawn; — adv. By means of, by reason of, through; in consequence of; on the part of, from
more: n. m. S. योग्य, Pr. joggo fit. 1. Conjunction of auspicious stars. 2. The nick of time; the auspicious moment. 3. Union with the Supreme being by means of abstract contemplation; religious meditation. Ye donoṅ jog sādhe baiṭhe hain . 4. A division of a great circle measured on the Ecliptic. 5. One in whose favor a bill is drawn. jog chāl , ( Ast .) Precession of the equinoxes. jog do-pahar chakkar , Equinoctial. jog sādhnā , v. n. To lead the life of an ascetic. jog lenā , v. n. To retire from the world; become a faqīr.
adv. On the part of ( taraf ); from.

adj. Fit; adequate; applicable; able; capable; proper; opportune; advisable. 1. Tan gudā-ke janam līyā, pī * bin rahī udās, Marne jogā kar dīyā, aisā kīyā masās . Riddle. Ans. Opium (or Love.) Double entendre . From body probed its birth, love-lorn (athirst) forlorn, To death's brink brought, by much attrition overborne. 2. Yeh muṅh pān jogā! Prov. Is this a mouth for pān? * 1. Drink. 2. A lover, spouse. † 1. Refuse of opium. 2. Like to. 3. Yeh marduā kis din kamāne jogā huā thā? Wom. When did this hulking fellow ever earn anything? jog-māyā , n. f. A goddess whose shrine is at the Kutab near Delhi.

s. m. ( योग ) 1. Junction, fitting. 2. A fortunate moment, opportunity, occasion. 3. Penance, devotion, religious meditation, union with the supreme being by means of abstract contemplation. 4. Certain divisions of a great circle, measured upon the Ecliptick. (See Asiat. Res. Vol. ix. p. 365). 5. ( योग्य ) adj. Possible, capable, fit. (In compos. it answers to able, worthy, &c.). جوگمايا jog-māyā, s. f. A deceptive power which Jogīs are supposed to possess.
subs: (S. yoga+abh˚), s.m. The manner of life and practice of Jogīs or Hindū ascetics
(S. yoga+āsana), s.m. The posture or mode of sitting proper for profound and abstract meditation
s.m. The precession of the equinoxes
v.n. To practise profound and abstract meditation; to lead the life of an ascetic
s.f. The practice or cultivation of religious abstraction
v.n. To retire from the world, to become an ascetic
s.f. The māyā or magical power of the Yoga, a certain illusionary power which Jogīs are supposed to possess (viz. the being able to assume at will any number of forms of their own body)
s.m. A kind of fish, Silurus ascita
s.f. A kind of sleep (supposed to be peculiar to devotees) which admits of the full exercise of the mental powers, a state of half-contemplation, half-sleep; light sleep, wakefulness
s.m. A menstruum or medium for mixing medicines; — s.f. Alkali; quicksilver

jogeś, jogeśvar (S. yogā+īś˚), s.m. A master of the Yoga; any principal sage or Jogī; one who has obtained superhuman faculties, a magician; a teacher of sacred science; a deity; the object of devout contemplation; an adorer, a devotee; — an epithet of Krishṅa, of Śiva, and of Yājnavalkya.

def: s.m. One who performs the kind of religious exercise called jog, a contemplative saint; a devotee, an ascetic, a hermit; one supposed to have obtained supernatural powers, a magician, a conjurer; — name of a particular mixed caste who are commonly weavers (this caste do not burn but bury their dead; and the women are sometimes buried alive with the corpses of their husbands).
more: n. m. S. योगी 1. A Hindu faqīr or ascetic; a devotee. ☞ Jogis do not burn, but bury their dead, and the women are sometimes buried alive with their husband's corpse. 1. Jogī terī sūnī manḍhyā kyoṅ? Rām Rām kare ram gaë jogī; dhūnī jūṅ kī tūṅ . Bhajan. O devotee! why is thy hut all still and silent now? With calling oft on Ram in Ram thy soul's absorbed, and lo! The sacred fire still burns as thou didst leave it here below! 2. Jogī jāt na pūchho, Rājah; Māran kūh, na gāran lājā , Padmāvat. Ask not, O King, of jogi's caste the name, Struck he's not hurt, abused he feels no shame. 2. A magician; conjuror.

s. m. 1. A Hindū faḳīr, a devotee. 2. A cast of Hindūs who are commonly weavers. (The people of this cast do not burn, but bury their dead; and, the women are sometimes buried alive with their husbandʼs corpse).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit योगिन् (yogin). Compare Punjabi ਜੋਗੀ (jogī), Bengali যোগী (jōgi), Marathi जोगी (jogī). Equivalent to جوگ (jog, “lit. union [with God], monastic renunciation, asceticism”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. The wife of a jogī; a female devotee or performer of jog.
more: E. n. f. The wife of a jogī. Nadī kināre dhuāṅ uṭhe, maiṅ jānūṅ kachhu hoë, Jā kāran jogan bhaī kahīṅ wohī na jaltā hoë . That rising smoke the river near—my heart misgives me sore, Not his the pyre sure for whom a Jogan I forlore!
s. f. Female of جوگي jogī, q. v.

def: s.f. A female demon or being of magical power (created by and attendant on Durgā), a witch, a sorceress; (in Astrol.) a spirit ruling periods of good and ill luck.
more: n. f. S. योगिनी 1. A female fiend or sprite, attendant on and created by Durga . 2. ( Astro .). Spirits ruling periods of good and ill-luck.
s. f. 1. A female fiend or sprite, attendant on and created by Durgā. 2. (in astrology) Spirits governing periods of good and ill luck.

def: s.f. (old H.) = joginī.
s.m. The colour worn by jogīs, a reddish-yellow colour; ochre (syn. geru); — name of a rāginī or musical mode; a kind of pigeon.
more: n. m. 1. Reddish yellow; ochre ( gerū ). Tore kāran, balamvā, chhāḍo des, ho kar līyo jogiyā bhes, Hāthoṅ bich sumran, gale bich selī, kāṅḍhe paṛe haiṅ kes . Wom. song. Birah. For you, my spouse, my home I've left, th' ascetic's garb put on, With beads in hand, black cord neck-hung, hair loose on shoulders flung. 2. A rāgnī or musical mode. Bajātī wo jogan jahāṅ jogiyā, To vāṅ baiṭhti khalq dhūnī lagā . Mīr Hasan. The world-renouncing maid upon her lyre played, A world attracted round, their sacred fires made.
s. m. 1. Name of a colour. 2. f. The name of a rāginī or musical mode. 3. A kind of pigeon.

def: s.f. Suitableness, propriety, fitness, appropriateness, consistency; ability, capability.
more: s. f. Suitableness, capability, ability, fitness.

def: s.m. (prov.) = jog, q.v. s.m. Joining, uniting, fitting; connection, union; yoking; putting to (horses); meeting, contact; mixture, combination, association; method, means, mode, manner; propriety, fitness, suitability; fixing (an arrow or other weapon); application of a remedy, remedy, cure; consequence, result; occasion, opportunity; (in Astron.) conjunction, a lucky conjuncture, auspicious or fortunate moment; (in Arith.) addition, sum, total; — application or concentration of the thoughts (keeping the body in a fixed posture); abstract contemplation, meditation, devotion, penance; union with the Supreme Being by means of abstract contemplation; the Yoga system of philosophy (as established by Patanjali, to teach the means by which the human soul may attain complete union with the Supreme Being); — the twenty-seventh part of a circle measured on the plane of the Ecliptic (and used in calculating the longitudes of the sun and moon); a division of the moon's path corresponding to the twenty-eight Nakshatras; (in banking) the person on whom a draft or bill of exchange is drawn; — adv. By means of, by reason of, through; in consequence of; on the part of, from
subs: (S. yoga+abh˚), s.m. The manner of life and practice of Jogīs or Hindū ascetics
(S. yoga+āsana), s.m. The posture or mode of sitting proper for profound and abstract meditation
s.m. The precession of the equinoxes
v.n. To practise profound and abstract meditation; to lead the life of an ascetic
s.f. The practice or cultivation of religious abstraction
v.n. To retire from the world, to become an ascetic
s.f. The māyā or magical power of the Yoga, a certain illusionary power which Jogīs are supposed to possess (viz. the being able to assume at will any number of forms of their own body)
s.m. A kind of fish, Silurus ascita
s.f. A kind of sleep (supposed to be peculiar to devotees) which admits of the full exercise of the mental powers, a state of half-contemplation, half-sleep; light sleep, wakefulness
s.m. A menstruum or medium for mixing medicines; — s.f. Alkali; quicksilver

jogeś, jogeśvar (S. yogā+īś˚), s.m. A master of the Yoga; any principal sage or Jogī; one who has obtained superhuman faculties, a magician; a teacher of sacred science; a deity; the object of devout contemplation; an adorer, a devotee; — an epithet of Krishṅa, of Śiva, and of Yājnavalkya.

def: adj. = jogā, q.v. adj. Useful, serviceable: fit, becoming, suitable proper, appropriate; apposite; applicable; fit for, qualified for; able, capable of, adequate; advisable; (in comp. jog answers to -able, -worthy, &c.; e.g. bar-jog, lit. 'fit for a bridegroom'; marriageable).
s.f. = jogtā, q.v. s.f. Suitableness, propriety, fitness, appropriateness, consistency; ability, capability.
s.m. Looking longingly at, gazing at, beholding
subs: adj. Worthy to behold, worth seeing (e.g. Mohan-kau mukha sohan, johan-jog).

def: v.t. To salute, to address reverentially, to make obeisance to.
adv. S. यहि Correl. of vohīṅ . As soon as; the very moment; no sooner. Johīṅ kahā, vohīṅ kīyā . No sooner said than done.

ety: joh˚ = Prk. जोएह(इ), or जोअ(इ), or जोए(इ); S. ज्योत्स्नय(ति), fr. ज्योत्स्न
def: v.t. To see, look at, behold, gaze at; to look longingly or eagerly at; to look for, expect, watch for ( = apekshā karnā; intiz̤ār karnā).
more: v. a. To expect, to look out for.

def: v. a. To look out for ( intizār k ).

ety: fr. justan; see jū or jo, and just
def: s.f. (used in comp.), Seeking, searching.
s.m. A measure of four kos, q.v.; a distance of from five to nine miles.
more: n. m. S. योजन Four kos , or eight miles. Jojan bhar tehīṅ badan pasārā, Kapi tan kīnh dugan bistārā . Rāmāyan. The monster full eight miles stretched out appeared, Ram's ape then twice that length his form upreared.
s. m. A measure of four cos (کوس kos, q. v.).

def: s.f. = joṅk, q.v. s.f. A leech
more: s. f. A leech. See جونک.

def: n. S. जुष to examine. Weight ( tol , 1); measurement.
s.f. Weighing; weight; measurement; a weight.
s.f. Weighing; measuring; the weighman's fee or perquisite ( = jukhāʼī).
more: n. f. 1. Weighment. 2. The weighman's fee.

def: adj. Weighed, determined by weighing; of weight.
v.n. To run the risk (of, -kī), to venture, risk, hazard; to suffer loss
more: s. f. Risk, venture, peril, danger, achievement, enterprise. جوکهم اٿهانا jokhim uṭhānā, To run the hazard, to venture, to risk.
subs: v.n. To run a risk; to be placed in jeopardy, to fall into danger
v.t. To risk; to place in jeopardy, to endanger.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit योगक्षेम (yogakṣema, “the security given by possessions, wealth, etc”), from योग (yoga, “junction, work”) + क्षेम (kṣema, “basis, security”). Compare Gujarati જોખમ (jokham), Marathi जोखीम (jokhīm).

def: adj. & s.m. Involving danger or risk, venturesome, perilous, hazardous; — a venturesome person, one who risks.
wety: From جوکھم (jokhim) + ـی (-ī).

def: v.t. To weigh; to measure; to estimate, rate, take the measure or amount of (one's abilities, &c.).
s.f. = jokhim, q.v. v.n. To run the risk (of, -kī), to venture, risk, hazard; to suffer loss
subs: v.n. To run a risk; to be placed in jeopardy, to fall into danger
v.t. To risk; to place in jeopardy, to endanger.

def: n. f. 1. Risk; hazard; stake; venture. 1. Kyoṅ kampo, kyoṅ thartharo, dhīraj kyoṅ na dharo? Dohā. Chākhā chāho pirem ras, jokhoṅ kyoṅ na saho? Why shake and tremble so? a stout heart you must bear, If love's sweets you would taste its danger you must share. 2. Kānī ke biyāh ko sau jokhoṅ . Prov. In marrying a one-eyed girl there are a hundred risks. 2. Fear; danger; jeopardy. 3. Any valuables, as jewels, money, etc. 4. Insurance ( bīmā ). jokhoṅ uṭhānā , v. n. To suffer loss. jokhoṅ kā kām , n. m. A dangerous affair; a hazardous enterprise. jokhoṅ meṅ paṛnā , v. n. To run a risk. jokhoṅ meṅ ḍālnā , v. n. To risk; endanger.
v.t. = jokhnā, q.v. v.t. To weigh; to measure; to estimate, rate, take the measure or amount of (one's abilities, &c.).

n. m. H. jāl net; Z. and P. jolāh ; A. julāhiq . 1. A weaver ( kolī ). 1. Julāhe kī jūtī, sipāhī kī joë, dharī dharī purānī hoë . Prov. A soldier's wife, a weaver's shoes, alike grow old for lack of use. 2. Āṭh julāhe, nau hukkā, tis par bhī thukkam thukkā . Prov. Nine hookkas 'mong eight weavers, see A hookkah fight amongst them free. ☞ Weavers are proverbially stupid. One story is that a crow, perched on the housetop, having carried off his child's bread, before giving him some more he took the precaution to remove the ladder. Another story is that, being told by a Mah. soothsayer ( rammāl ) it was written in his fate that his nose would be cut off by an axe, the simple weaver was incredulous, and, taking up an axe he kept moving it saying—' Yūṅ karbā to goṛ kaṭbā, yoṅ karbā to hāth kaṭbā, aur yoṅ karbā tab nā '—'If I do so I cut my leg, if I do so I cut my hand, but unless I do so—(His nose was off). 3. Jolhā jāne jau kāṭe? Can a weaver cut barley? ☞ The story goes that a weaver, unable to pay his debt, was set to cut the ripe barley by his agriculturist creditor who thought to repay himself in this way. Instead of reaping he was continually untwisting the tangled barley stems. 4. Jolhā chorābathi narī narī, Khodā chorābathi ekke berī . Tir. Prov. The weaver steals a reel at a time, God has him robbed at one swoop (whole pieces of cloth). 2. A fool; blockhead. 3. An aquatic insect. julāhe kī sī dāṛhī , n. f. A short, pointed French beard, like a weaver's. chamar-julāhā , W.; tāṅtī julāhā , E. n. m. An inferior caste of weavers.
s.f. Partner, fellow, companion, &c. = joṛī, q.v. (used chiefly in comp, e.g. ham-jolī, q.v.).

ety: Prk. जवडु (by change of ḍ to n), fr. जेव, fr. S. यावत्
def: adv. As, like; when ( = jo and jab, q.v.)
subs: adv. As before, in statu quo; in some way or other, anyhow
jon-tyoṅ kar or karke, adv. By some means or other, by any means, somehow, anyhow; as it could be done; with much difficulty

jūṅ-jūṅ, adv. As; as far as, as long as; as by degrees; — joṅ-kā toṅ, jūṅ-kī tūṅ, adj. & adv. Unaltered, untouched, perfect, whole, the very same; — as originally, as it was, in statu quo, the same as ever, precisely the same; as in fact, as in reality
v.t. To preserve or keep unaltered; to let alone; to keep safe and sound; to maintain intact, to uphold
adv. As, like; as possible, in any way
joṅ-hīṅ, adv. The very moment that, just as soon as, no sooner; just as fast as; exactly as if.
wety: Borrowed from English June, ultimately from Latin Iunius.

def: s.m.f. The womb, uterus, vulva, vagina, pudendum mulieris
subs: (S. yoni+arśas), s.m. A fleshy excrescence in the vulva, menorrhagia or prolapsus uteri
s.f. The upper part of the vulva, the point of union of the labiœ.
wety: Borrowed from English June, ultimately from Latin Iunius.

def: v.t. = jownā = johnā, q.v. v.t. To see, look at, behold, regard.
